title: Zuplo API keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Zuplo API key detected. API Keys provide authenticated access to Zuplo's API gateway platform for managing routes, rate limiting, authentication policies, and API configurations. Exposing API keys allows attackers to modify gateway routing rules, bypass rate limits, access API analytics, manipulate authentication policies, and potentially compromise all APIs proxied through your Zuplo gateway. Immediately revoke the compromised key from the Zuplo dashboard, regenerate new credentials, and audit all gateway configuration changes for unauthorized modifications.

Zuplo Bearer token detected. Bearer tokens in Authorization headers provide direct authenticated access to Zuplo's API gateway management endpoints. Exposing Bearer tokens enables attackers to reconfigure gateway routes, modify rate limiting policies, access API analytics and logs, manipulate authentication rules, and compromise the entire API gateway infrastructure. Immediately invalidate the exposed token through the Zuplo dashboard, regenerate new credentials with minimal required permissions, and review all recent gateway configuration changes and API access logs for unauthorized activity.

Zuplo API key assignment detected. Direct assignment of API keys or Bearer tokens in source code exposes your Zuplo API gateway credentials. These credentials control gateway routing, rate limiting, authentication policies, and API access management. Compromised keys enable attackers to reconfigure your entire API gateway, bypass security policies, access sensitive API analytics, and potentially expose all backend APIs. Use environment variables or secure secret management systems to store Zuplo credentials and implement proper key rotation policies.
